name: Risk/Reward Room
description: Risk/Reward Room is a run-structure mechanic that supports replayability through randomness, reset, risk, or synergy.
design_notes: Risk/Reward Room should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For roguelike mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use risk/reward room as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect risk/reward room to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels each run is a fresh risk with potential for unexpected synergy.

parameters.name: run_length_target
parameters.description: Expected duration of one run segment using the mechanic.

parameters.name: randomness_weight
parameters.description: How strongly random selection affects outcomes.

parameters.name: carryover_amount
parameters.description: How much state persists between runs.

edge_cases: Risk/Reward Room is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Risk/Reward Room overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Risk/Reward Room runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Risk/Reward Room state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Risk/Reward Room plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Risk/Reward Room produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Risk/Reward Room strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Risk/Reward Room becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Risk/Reward Room with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Risk/Reward Room as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Risk/Reward Room with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Risk/Reward Room as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
